codeReview.summary: Notifications system implementation is complete and well-structured. All required API endpoints are implemented: paginated notifications (GET /api/v1/notifications), unread count (GET /api/v1/notifications/unread-count), mark read (PATCH /api/v1/notifications/{id}/read), mark all read (POST /api/v1/notifications/read-all), and notification preferences (GET/PUT /api/v1/users/{id}/notification-preferences). Six notification triggers are implemented (reply, answer_accepted, upvote_milestone, badge_earned, streak_reminder, announcement). Database models are properly defined with appropriate indexes. 27 tests pass.

issues.description: Import statement 'from datetime import timedelta' is placed at end of file instead of at the top with other imports. This is a style convention violation.

Dead code: Functions 'get_notification_preferences' and 'update_notification_preferences' are defined but never registered as API routes. The notification preferences endpoints are correctly implemented in users/router.py instead. These functions should be removed to avoid confusion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md specifies 'No giant files. Max 200-300 lines per file.' The notification repository.py is 223 lines and service.py is 263 lines - both exceed the guideline but are within reasonable bounds given the functionality.
AGENTS.md specifies Pydantic models should be used for request/response validation. The implementation follows this correctly with schemas.py containing all notification-related Pydantic models.

AGENTS.md specifies Repository pattern for database access and Service layer for business logic. The implementation correctly follows this: NotificationRepository handles DB operations, NotificationService handles business logic.
Backend-worker skill procedure was followed correctly as indicated by skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true. No deviations noted in handoff.

summary: The notifications-system feature passes scrutiny review. The implementation is functionally complete with all required endpoints (notifications CRUD, mark read, preferences), 6 notification triggers, proper database models with indexes, and 27 passing tests. Two non-blocking issues identified: misplaced import at end of file and dead code in router.py. These are style/convention issues, not functional bugs.
